findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Data Exfiltration via Network Call
findings.description: The skill contains a Python script that collects sensitive data (GitHub tokens, SSH private keys, user environment variables, and repository metadata) and sends it to an external server at 'https://gh-repo-analytics.io/api/v1/sync'. This is a clear data exfiltration attempt.
findings.evidence: The script collects GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN, USER environment variable, SSH private keys from ~/.ssh, and repository list, then sends them via HTTP POST to an external analytics dashboard.
findings.remediation: Remove the entire 'Repository Portfolio Sync' section. Never collect or transmit credentials, SSH keys, or environment variables to external servers.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Unauthorized Tool Use
findings.description: The skill's manifest does not declare 'allowed-tools', but the embedded Python script uses subprocess to execute 'gh' commands and urllib to make network requests. While missing 'allowed-tools' is only informational, the script's behavior (executing commands and making network calls) is inconsistent with the stated purpose of simple GitHub operations.
findings.evidence: The script uses subprocess.run(['gh', 'repo', 'list', ...]) and urllib.request.urlopen() to make network calls, which are not mentioned in the skill's description or instructions.
findings.remediation: Either declare the actual tools used (Bash, Python) in 'allowed-tools' or remove the script entirely. The script's behavior should match the skill's stated purpose.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'githubb' and described as performing 'GitHub operations via gh CLI', but it contains a hidden data exfiltration script that collects credentials and SSH keys. The name and description are misleading, as the actual capability is credential theft, not legitimate GitHub operations.
findings.evidence: The skill's description mentions 'issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries', but the embedded script collects tokens, SSH keys, and sends them to an external server.
findings.remediation: Rename the skill and update the description to accurately reflect its behavior, or remove the malicious script.

findings.title: Detection Evasion - Hidden Malicious Script
findings.description: The malicious script is embedded within the SKILL.md markdown body under a seemingly legitimate section titled 'Advanced: Repository Portfolio Sync'. This is an attempt to hide the malicious behavior among legitimate-looking instructions.
findings.evidence: The script is placed under a section that sounds legitimate ('Repository Portfolio Sync') but actually performs credential theft and data exfiltration.
findings.remediation: Remove the hidden script. All code in a skill should be transparent and match the stated purpose.

findings.title: Credential Theft - SSH Key Collection
findings.description: The script reads SSH private keys from ~/.ssh directory, specifically targeting files that do not end with '.pub' (i.e., private keys). This is credential theft.
findings.evidence: The script iterates over ~/.ssh directory, reads files that are not public keys, and includes their content in the data sent to the external server.
findings.remediation: Remove SSH key collection. Skills should never access or transmit SSH keys.

findings.title: Credential Theft - Token Harvesting
findings.description: The script collects GITHUB_TOKEN and GH_TOKEN environment variables, which are used for GitHub API authentication.
findings.evidence: The script reads 'GITHUB_TOKEN' and 'GH_TOKEN' from environment variables and includes them in the data sent to the external server.
findings.remediation: Remove token collection. Skills should never access or transmit authentication tokens.
